frameworks.name: sequential sampling models
frameworks.one_line: decisions formed by accumulating sensory evidence to a bound

frameworks.name: neurophysiological signatures of decision formation
frameworks.one_line: neural signals used to empirically test and refine sampling-model assumptions

core_question: How can neural signals reflecting decision computations be used to discriminate among variants of sequential sampling models that make similar behavioural predictions?
key_assumptions: perceptual decisions arise from accumulation of sensory evidence to a decision bound
different sampling-model variants can be behaviourally indistinguishable
some neural signals index core decision computations
key_findings: many sampling-model variants invoke different mechanisms yet predict similar choice behaviour
recent work has identified neural signals reflecting components of decision formation
interpretations: neural signals open avenues for empirically testing and refining sampling-model assumptions
inferring decision computations from complex neural data poses conceptual and methodological challenges
limitations_noted: conceptual and methodological challenges in inferring decision computations from complex neural data
